mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >65% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based demand recovery, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both increase, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a sustained rise in the index of industrial production reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to generate positive 5-day forward returns during tightening cycles, with a hit rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of months where CPI inflation exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment yields negative forward returns in >55% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth against 60-day forward returns remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A breach of the industrial production index below its six-month trend signals a supply-side contraction that precedes earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO drops below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >58% of instances where industrial production falls below its 6-month moving average.
